# UC-22.5.15 · MiFID II Conflicts of Interest — Personal Account Dealing Near Client Block Trades

## Description

Conflicts policies require surveillance of staff dealing. Correlating employee PA trades with adjacent client block orders in the same instruments surfaces potential front-running or misuse of confidential information.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes surveillance, trading and sourcetypes oms:block, pad:trade. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tune the 900-second proximity window per liquidity and venue; (2) same-side matching surfaces potential front-running—adjust to opposite-side if your policy targets different abuse patterns; (3) enforce strict RBAC on `staff_id`; (4) escalate hits to compliance investigations with underlying fills retained under legal hold outside Splunk; (5) document methodology in the conflicts register. Indexes required: `index=surveillance`, `index=trading`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=oms:block`, `sourcetype=pad:trade`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Compliance trade surveillance platform via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=surveillance` `sourcetype="pad:trade"` (staff_id, symbol, side, qty, trade_ts); `index=trading` `sourcetype="oms:block"` (symbol, side, qty, block_ts, client_segment).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tune the 900-second proximity window per liquidity and venue; (2) same-side matching surfaces potential front-running—adjust to opposite-side if your policy targets different abuse patterns; (3) enforce strict RBAC on `staff_id`; (4) escalate hits to compliance investigations with underlying fills retained under legal hold outside Splunk; (5) document methodology in the conflicts register.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=surveillance sourcetype="pad:trade" earliest=-90d
| rename side as pad_side
| eval t=strptime(trade_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left symbol [
    search index=trading sourcetype="oms:block" earliest=-90d
    | rename side as block_side
    | eval bt=strptime(block_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | fields symbol, bt, block_side, qty
  ]
| eval delta=abs(t-bt)
| where delta<900 AND pad_side=block_side
| table staff_id, symbol, pad_side, t, bt, delta, qty
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Conflicts of Interest — Personal Account Dealing Near Client Block Trades** — Conflicts policies require surveillance of staff dealing. Correlating employee PA trades with adjacent client block orders in the same instruments surfaces potential front-running or misuse of confidential information.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=surveillance` `sourcetype="pad:trade"` (staff_id, symbol, side, qty, trade_ts); `index=trading` `sourcetype="oms:block"` (symbol, side, qty, block_ts, client_segment). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Compliance trade surveillance platform via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: surveillance; **sourcetype**: pad:trade. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=surveillance, sourcetype="pad:trade", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **t** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where delta<900 AND pad_side=block_side` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: block_side, bt, delta, pad_side, qty, staff_id, symbol, t.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hits), Timeline, Single value (staff_id count).

## SPL

```spl
index=surveillance sourcetype="pad:trade" earliest=-90d
| rename side as pad_side
| eval t=strptime(trade_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left symbol [
    search index=trading sourcetype="oms:block" earliest=-90d
    | rename side as block_side
    | eval bt=strptime(block_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | fields symbol, bt, block_side, qty
  ]
| eval delta=abs(t-bt)
| where delta<900 AND pad_side=block_side
| table staff_id, symbol, pad_side, t, bt, delta, qty
```

## Visualization

Table (hits), Timeline, Single value (staff_id count).
